filename: Philip K. Dick - Lies, Inc. (2007, Knopf Doubleday Publishing Group) - libgen.li.epub
display_title: Lies, Inc.
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: An expanded version of Dick's 1966 novel The Unteleported Man, incorporating hallucinatory material removed from the original publication. The novel concerns teleportation to a colony planet and a conspiracy to conceal the truth about conditions there. Published posthumously in 1984. Knopf Doubleday 2007 EPUB edition.

filename: The Crack in Space{Dick, Philip K.}(2007, Knopf Doubleday Publishing Group){114429853} libgen.li.epub
display_title: The Crack in Space (Knopf Doubleday Edition)
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A 1966 novel about an overpopulated Earth where a repairman discovers a crack in a Jiffi-scuttler appliance that opens a portal to a parallel Earth. Political machinations follow as factions compete to exploit the new world. Knopf Doubleday 2007 EPUB edition.

filename: The Crack in Space{Philip K. Dick}(1977, Mariner Books){114663826} libgen.li.epub
display_title: The Crack in Space (Mariner Books Edition)
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: The Mariner Books 1977 edition of Dick's 1966 novel about a portal to a parallel Earth discovered inside a malfunctioning appliance. Duplicate of the Knopf Doubleday edition also in the archive. EPUB format.

filename: The Ganymede Takeover{Philip K. Dick &amp_&amp_ Ray Nelson}(1967, ACE BOOKS){112999899} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: The Ganymede Takeover
author: Philip K. Dick and Ray Nelson
card_summary: A collaborative novel by Dick and his friend Ray Nelson (1967), set on an Earth conquered by worm-like aliens from Ganymede. Human resistance forces use psychedelic drugs, music, and guerrilla tactics against the occupiers. The only novel Dick co-wrote with Nelson, his Berkeley friend and fellow SF writer. Ace Books edition, 164 pages.

filename: [Ace Double D-193 ] Dick, Philip K - The Man Who Japed (1956, Ace) - libgen.li.cbr
display_title: The Man Who Japed (Ace Double D-193)
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: The original 1956 Ace Double edition of Dick's satirical novel about a future moralistic society where Allen Purcell, a propaganda agency director, discovers he has been unconsciously vandalizing the statue of the regime's founder. An early exploration of repression and subversion. CBR (comic book archive) format scan of the paperback.

filename: [Extrapolation 2007-jan vol. 48 iss. 2] The French Dick_ Villiers de l&_039_Isle-Adam, Philip K. Dick, and the Android{Calvin, Ritch}(2007 January)[10.3828_extr.2007.48.2.9]{66956321} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: The French Dick: Villiers de l'Isle-Adam, Philip K. Dick, and the Android
author: Ritch Calvin
card_summary: A scholarly article from Extrapolation (Vol. 48, No. 2, January 2007) examining the literary connection between French symbolist writer Villiers de l'Isle-Adam and Philip K. Dick through the figure of the android. Calvin traces how both authors explored artificial beings as vehicles for questioning authenticity and humanity. 24 pages.

filename: [Extrapolation 2011-jan vol. 52 iss. 2] The Holy Family from Outer Space_ Reconsidering Philip K. Dick&_039_s The Divine Inva...{Rossi, Umberto}(2011 January)[10.3828_extr.2011.52.2.2]{66776860} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: The Holy Family from Outer Space: Reconsidering Philip K. Dick's The Divine Invasion
author: Umberto Rossi
card_summary: A scholarly article from Extrapolation (Vol. 52, No. 2, January 2011) in which Umberto Rossi reconsiders Dick's novel The Divine Invasion through the concept of the holy family. Rossi, a prominent Italian Dick scholar, examines the theological and familial structures in one of Dick's most overtly religious late novels. 21 pages.

filename: [Game ] Dick, Philip Kindred - Players of Titan - libgen.li.pdf
display_title: The Game-Players of Titan (Third Edition)
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A third PDF edition of Dick's 1963 novel about telepathic aliens from Titan overseeing a high-stakes board game on a depopulated Earth. 83 pages in this edition. Duplicate of other Game-Players editions in the archive.

filename: [HMH] Philip K. Dick - Radio Free Albemuth (2020, HMH Books_ Houghton Mifflin Harcourt) - libgen.li.epub
display_title: Radio Free Albemuth
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: Dick's original, more autobiographical version of VALIS, written in 1976 but published posthumously in 1985. The novel features a character named Philip K. Dick alongside his friend Nicholas Brady, who receives transmissions from an alien satellite called VALIS. The most directly autobiographical of Dick's novels. HMH Books 2020 EPUB edition.

filename: [Organization 1999-nov vol. 6 iss. 4] Managers as Androids_ Reading Moral Agency in Philip Dick{Srinivas, N.}(1999 November)[10.1177_135050849964003]{46764688} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: Managers as Androids: Reading Moral Agency in Philip Dick
author: N. Srinivas
card_summary: A scholarly article from Organization (Vol. 6, No. 4, November 1999) reading Dick's android fiction through the lens of organizational theory and moral agency. Srinivas uses Dick's exploration of human-android boundaries to interrogate corporate management and the dehumanization of workplace relations. 16 pages.

filename: [Oxford Bookworms Library_ Stage 5 - Oxford Bookworms Library_ Stage 5] Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep_{Philip K. Dick &amp_ Andy Hopkins &amp_ Joc Potter}(2007, OUP Oxford){113916706} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep? (Oxford Bookworms Adaptation)
author: Philip K. Dick (adapted by Andy Hopkins and Joc Potter)
card_summary: An adapted/simplified version of Dick's classic novel for the Oxford Bookworms Library (Stage 5, advanced level). Adapted by Andy Hopkins and Joc Potter for English language learners, published by Oxford University Press in 2007. Demonstrates the global educational reach of Dick's most famous novel. 183 pages.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies 1980-jul vol. 7 iss. 2] The Encounter of Taoism and Fascism in Philip K. Dick&_039_s &quot_The Man in the High Castle&quot_ (La rencontr...{Patricia Warrick}(1980 July)[10.2307_4239329]{332794.pdf
display_title: The Encounter of Taoism and Fascism in The Man in the High Castle
author: Patricia Warrick
card_summary: A foundational scholarly article from Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 7, No. 2, July 1980) by Patricia Warrick examining the philosophical tension between Taoism and fascism in Dick's The Man in the High Castle. Warrick was one of the earliest and most important academic Dick scholars. 18 pages.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies 1983-jul vol. 10 iss. 2] Reality as Ideological Construct_ A Reading of Five Novels by Philip K. Dick (De la réalité comme...{Peter Fitting}(1983 July)[10.2307_4239550]{33279680} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: Reality as Ideological Construct: A Reading of Five Novels by Philip K. Dick
author: Peter Fitting
card_summary: A Marxist-informed scholarly article from Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 10, No. 2, July 1983) reading five Dick novels through the lens of ideology and constructed reality. Fitting examines how Dick's fictional realities expose the ideological construction of consensus reality. 19 pages.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies 1984-mar vol. 11 iss. 1] Towards a Theory of Paranoia_ The Science Fiction of Philip K. Dick (Vers une théorie de la paran...{Carl Freedman}(1984 March)[10.2307_4239584]{33271115} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: Towards a Theory of Paranoia: The Science Fiction of Philip K. Dick
author: Carl Freedman
card_summary: An influential scholarly article from Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 11, No. 1, March 1984) in which Carl Freedman develops a theoretical framework for understanding paranoia as a structural element in Dick's fiction. Freedman later became one of the leading theorists of science fiction. 11 pages.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies 1988-jul vol. 15 iss. 2] Philip K. Dick __ Between Faith and Melancholy_ Irony and the Gnostic Meaning of Dick&_039_s &quot_Divine T...{Jean-Noël Dumont , Danièle Chatelain and George Slusser}.pdf
display_title: Between Faith and Melancholy: Irony and the Gnostic Meaning of Dick's Divine Trilogy
author: Jean-Noel Dumont (trans. Daniele Chatelain and George Slusser)
card_summary: A scholarly article from the special Philip K. Dick issue of Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 15, No. 2, July 1988) examining irony and Gnostic theology in Dick's VALIS trilogy. Translated from French by Chatelain and Slusser, reflecting the strong French academic tradition of Dick scholarship. 4 pages.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies 1988-jul vol. 15 iss. 2] Philip K. Dick __ Dick in France_ A Love Story (Dick en France_ une histoire d&_039_amour){Roger Bozzetto, Danièle Chatelain , George Slusser and R. M. P.}(1988 July)[.pdf
display_title: Dick in France: A Love Story
author: Roger Bozzetto (trans. Daniele Chatelain, George Slusser, and R.M.P.)
card_summary: A scholarly article from the special PKD issue of Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 15, No. 2, July 1988) chronicling the reception and influence of Dick's work in France, where he achieved literary celebrity status far exceeding his American reputation. Translated from French. 11 pages.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies 1988-jul vol. 15 iss. 2] Philip K. Dick __ Dick, the Libertarian Prophet (Dick_ une prophète libertaire){Daniel Fondanèche , Danièle Chatelain and George Slusser}(1988 July)[10.2307_4239877]{3.pdf
display_title: Dick, the Libertarian Prophet
author: Daniel Fondaneche (trans. Daniele Chatelain and George Slusser)
card_summary: A scholarly article from the special PKD issue of Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 15, No. 2, July 1988) examining Dick as a prophetic voice for libertarian themes. Translated from French, reflecting the vigorous French critical engagement with Dick's political and philosophical dimensions. 12 pages.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies 1988-jul vol. 15 iss. 2] Philip K. Dick __ Editorial Introduction_ Philip K. Dick and Criticism{Carl Freedman}(1988 July)[10.2307_4239875]{33271374} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: Editorial Introduction: Philip K. Dick and Criticism
author: Carl Freedman
card_summary: Carl Freedman's editorial introduction to the special Philip K. Dick issue of Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 15, No. 2, July 1988), surveying the state of Dick criticism and framing the collected articles. This landmark special issue helped establish Dick as a major subject of academic literary study. 11 pages.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies 1988-jul vol. 15 iss. 2] Philip K. Dick __ Irrational Expectations_ or, How Economics and the Post-Industrial World Failed...{Eric S. Rabkin}(1988 July)[10.2307_4239879]{33271377} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: Irrational Expectations: How Economics and the Post-Industrial World Failed Philip K. Dick
author: Eric S. Rabkin
card_summary: A scholarly article from the special PKD issue of Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 15, No. 2, July 1988) by Eric S. Rabkin examining Dick's relationship with economics and the post-industrial world. Explores how economic precarity shaped Dick's fiction and worldview. 13 pages.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies 1988-jul vol. 15 iss. 2] Philip K. Dick __ P.K. Dick_ From the Death of the Subject to a Theology of Late Capitalism (P.K....{Scott Durham}(1988 July)[10.2307_4239880]{33216140} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: P.K. Dick: From the Death of the Subject to a Theology of Late Capitalism
author: Scott Durham
card_summary: A theoretically dense scholarly article from the special PKD issue of Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 15, No. 2, July 1988) connecting Dick's fiction to postmodern theory, tracing a trajectory from the death of the subject to a theological response to late capitalism. 15 pages.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies 1988-jul vol. 15 iss. 2] Philip K. Dick __ Philip K. Dick_ Authenticity and Insincerity (Philip K. Dick_ l&_039_authentique et ...{John Huntington}(1988 July)[10.2307_4239878]{33271376} li (1).pdf
display_title: Philip K. Dick: Authenticity and Insincerity
author: John Huntington
card_summary: A scholarly article from the special PKD issue of Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 15, No. 2, July 1988) examining the tension between authenticity and insincerity in Dick's fiction. Huntington explores how Dick's characters navigate genuine and counterfeit experience. 10 pages.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies 1991-jul vol. 18 iss. 2] Dick, Deception, and Dissociation_ A Comment on &quot_The Two Faces of Philip K. Dick&quot_{Gregg Rickman}(1991 July)[10.2307_4240073]{33279793} libgen.li (1).pdf
display_title: Dick, Deception, and Dissociation: A Comment on 'The Two Faces of Philip K. Dick'
author: Gregg Rickman
card_summary: A response article from Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 18, No. 2, July 1991) by Gregg Rickman, Dick's biographer, commenting on Robert Philmus's article 'The Two Faces of Philip K. Dick.' Rickman brings his deep biographical knowledge to bear on questions of deception and dissociation in Dick's life and work. 5 pages.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies 1991-mar vol. 18 iss. 1] The Two Faces of Philip K. Dick (Les deux visages de Philip K. Dick){Robert M. Philmus}(1991 March)[10.2307_4240032]{33279755} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: The Two Faces of Philip K. Dick
author: Robert M. Philmus
card_summary: A scholarly article from Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 18, No. 1, March 1991) examining duality and contradictions in Dick's literary persona and work. Philmus's article prompted a response from biographer Gregg Rickman in a subsequent issue. 14 pages.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies 2008-mar vol. 35 iss. 1] Errata_ L&_039_oscuro scutare di Philip K. Dick (Philip K. Dick&_039_s Dark Scanning){Review by_ Umberto Rossi}(2008 March)[10.2307_25475127]{33275697} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: Errata: L'oscuro scrutare di Philip K. Dick (Review)
author: Umberto Rossi
card_summary: A review/errata notice from Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 35, No. 1, March 2008) by Umberto Rossi regarding his Italian-language study of Dick, L'oscuro scrutare di Philip K. Dick (Philip K. Dick's Dark Scanning). Reflects the international dimension of Dick scholarship. 2 pages.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies vol. 43 iss. 2] The I Ching and Philip K. Dick&_039_s The Man in the High Castle{Paul Mountfort,}(2016)[10.5621_sciefictstud.43.2.0287]{61719871} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: The I Ching and Philip K. Dick's The Man in the High Castle
author: Paul Mountfort
card_summary: A scholarly article from Science Fiction Studies (Vol. 43, No. 2, 2016) examining Dick's use of the I Ching in writing and structuring The Man in the High Castle. Mountfort explores how the ancient Chinese oracle functioned as both narrative device and compositional method. 24 pages.

filename: [The Missouri Review vol. 7 iss. 2] The Mainstream That Through the Ghetto Flows _ An Interview with Philip K. Dick{Dick, Philip K.}(1984)[10.1353_mis.1984.0010]{78879153} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: The Mainstream That Through the Ghetto Flows: An Interview with Philip K. Dick
author: Philip K. Dick (interviewee)
card_summary: An interview with Philip K. Dick published in The Missouri Review (Vol. 7, No. 2, 1984). The title references Dick's lifelong tension between science fiction (the 'ghetto') and mainstream literary ambitions. One of the most substantial literary interviews Dick gave, published posthumously. 23 pages.

filename: [Theory Culture &amp_ Society 2008-dec vol. 25 iss. 7-8] Machines Making Gods_ Philip K. Dick, Henri Bergson and Saint Paul{Burton, J.}(2008 December)[10.1177_0263276408097807]{47184890} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: Machines Making Gods: Philip K. Dick, Henri Bergson and Saint Paul
author: James Burton
card_summary: A scholarly article from Theory, Culture & Society (Vol. 25, No. 7-8, December 2008) connecting Dick's fiction to the philosophy of Henri Bergson and the theology of Saint Paul. Burton, who later wrote a full monograph on Dick and Bergson, examines the relationship between technology, divinity, and creative evolution. 23 pages.

filename: [Utopian Studies vol. 7 iss. 2] Search for Philip K. Dick, 1928-1982_ A Memoir and Biography of the Science Fiction Writerby Anne...{Review by_ Michael J. Tolley}(1996)[10.2307_20719535]{55057604} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: Review: Search for Philip K. Dick, 1928-1982 by Anne Dick
author: Michael J. Tolley
card_summary: A book review from Utopian Studies (Vol. 7, No. 2, 1996) by Michael J. Tolley reviewing Anne Dick's memoir and biography Search for Philip K. Dick, 1928-1982. Anne Dick was Philip's third wife, and her account provides intimate biographical details from their marriage during the early 1960s. 3 pages.

filename: [aka Lies, Inc.] Philip K. Dick - The Unteleported Man (1983, Berkley) - libgen.li.pdf
display_title: The Unteleported Man (aka Lies, Inc.)
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: The 1983 Berkley edition of Dick's novel about Rachmael ben Applebaum, who suspects that teleportation to the colony planet Whale's Mouth is a one-way trap. Originally a 1966 novella expanded into a novel with hallucinatory additional material. Later retitled Lies, Inc. 153 pages.

filename: [Études littéraires 2005-apr 12 vol. 30 iss. 1] Le réel attrapé par l'imaginaire _ Philip Dick et la science-fonctionnalisation de la réalité{Saint-Gelais, Richard}(2005 April 12)[10.7202_501190ar]{68441196} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: Le reel attrape par l'imaginaire: Philip Dick et la science-fonctionnalisation de la realite
author: Richard Saint-Gelais
card_summary: A French-language scholarly article from Etudes litteraires (Vol. 30, No. 1, April 2005) examining how Dick's fiction captures and transforms reality through the imaginary. Saint-Gelais explores the concept of 'science-functionalization' of reality in Dick's work. Part of the strong Francophone tradition of Dick scholarship. 15 pages.
